title: DeltaCert Certificate
description: Schema for DeltaCert certificate JSON artifacts, produced by deltacert.py::certify() and collectors.py::certify_from_layers(). Both cert-builder code paths emit this same shape. A cert may optionally carry a `signature` block (see deltacert.signing) making it a signed, tamper-evident artifact.

model.description: Model identifier this certificate was issued for.

description.description: Free-text description of the certified configuration.

certified.description: True only if every layer in `layers` independently certified.

threshold_d.description: Default/reference d_COMM threshold. Per-layer `budget` (inside each layers[*] entry) is the authoritative gating value actually used.

formula.description: Human-readable statement of the d_COMM formula used to compute this certificate.

theorem.description: Citation for the theoretical result the divergence bound is derived from.

validation_status.description: flagship_validated: every collector behind this cert has a real hardware run with a measured downstream benchmark published in the DeltaCert paper. implemented_pending_validation: at least one layer's collector is implemented and tested but has not been run through that end-to-end validation process yet. This field is signed as part of the certificate payload — it cannot be stripped without invalidating the signature, so a signature can never be used to make an unvalidated measurement look more trustworthy than it is.

layers.description: One entry per certified layer/collector, keyed by collector name.

metadata.description: Environment/provenance stamps. Shape varies slightly by collection path (see _build_metadata / _environment_metadata in the DeltaCert source) but both include at minimum certified_at and deltacert_version where available.

signature.description: Present only if the certificate has been signed (deltacert sign). Its own value is computed over the certificate with this field excluded — see deltacert.signing.canonicalize.
